Title: A natural allele of the transcription factor gene TaMYB-D7b is a genetic signature for phosphorus deficiency in wheat

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Visible pigmentation associated with phosphorus and nitrogen deficiency responses
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream to midstream, likely early committed flavonoid step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of purple pigments
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes condensation of p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to generate a chalcone precursor for anthocyanidin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher TaCHSL2 expression is associated with purple pigment accumulation; dramatic downregulation in TaMYB7-edited wheat correlates with loss of purple color
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaCHSL2 homoeologs encode CHS 2-like proteins highly similar to chalcone synthases involved in flavonoid and floral pigment biosynthesis. 2. TaCHSL2 promoters contain multiple predicted TaMYB-D7 binding motifs. 3. TaCHSL2 transcript levels were dramatically reduced in TaMYB7-edited plants lacking purple pigmentation, supporting a role in anthocyanidin biosynthesis and metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated with P supply; downregulated in TaMYB7-edited plants under severe stress; very low in Jagger
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves/shoot tissues assayed by qPCR; induced with P supply in 2174
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphorus supply; nitrogen supply; severe nutrient stress; no N and P
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR expression analysis in wild type vs TaMYB7-edited wheat; promoter motif analysis; transient transcription assay with TaCHSL-A2 promoter; correlation with pigment phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native TaCHSL-A2 promoter in wheat; 1.6-kb TaCHSL-A2 promoter used as reporter in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream metabolic gene for engineering purple pigmentation / anthocyanidin accumulation, likely most effective when combined with functional TaMYB-D7b
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: QTL mapping with SSR and Illumina Infinium 9K iSelect SNP genotyping; qPCR-based expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is inferred as 'may be involved' in anthocyanidin biosynthesis and metabolism; direct enzyme activity was not biochemically assayed in this study; regulation by TaMYB7 and other genetic factors is suggested.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: 2174 allele TaMYB-D7b; Jagger allele TaMYB-D7a; Chinese Spring allele TaMYB-D7c
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Visible pigmentation diagnostic of phosphorus deficiency and also responsive to nitrogen status; potential indicator of nutrient-use status
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB domain protein; R3-type MYB; helix-turn-helix DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TaCHSL2 homoeologs; specifically TaCHSL-A2 promoter validated
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: TaMYB-D7b activates TaCHSL2 transcription; TaMYB-D7a shows little or no activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major regulator controlling purple pigmentation in wheat seedlings and leaves; functional TaMYB-D7b allele is decisive for purple pigment accumulation under nutrient deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional TaMYB-D7b promotes purple pigment accumulation in coleoptiles and leaves, especially under low P; nonfunctional natural allele TaMYB-D7a and CRISPR knockouts abolish purple pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QTL mapping identified a major locus on chromosome 7D associated with purple leaves, and TraesCS7D02G166500/TaMYB-D7 was prioritized as the causal gene. 2. TaMYB-D7b from cv. 2174 differs from TaMYB-D7a from Jagger by a single Ser/Gly substitution at amino acid 50 and has stronger DNA-binding and transcriptional activation of TaCHSL-A2. 3. CRISPR editing of TaMYB7 homoeologs in cv. 2174 abolished purple pigment accumulation throughout the life cycle and dramatically reduced TaCHSL2 expression. 4. TaMYB-D7 expression is upregulated by P supply and downregulated by N supply. 5. TaMYB-D7b is useful as a genetic signature and breeding marker for phosphorus deficiency responses in wheat.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TaMYB-D7 transcript level is about twofold higher in 2174 than in Jagger; upregulated by P supply; downregulated by N supply; downregulated in edited plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves/shoot tissues at seedling and later vegetative stages; associated with purple tissues under nutrient stress
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Phosphorus supply positively affects expression; nitrogen supply negatively affects expression; nutrient deficiency state
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible positive feedback/autoregulation inferred because TaMYB-D7b transcript levels were significantly downregulated in TaMYB-D7b-edited plants
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TaCHSL2 promoter DNA element; other protein partners not experimentally identified
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; allele sequencing; qPCR; yeast transactivation assay; EMSA; transient dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; CRISPR/Cas9 editing of TaMYB7 homoeologs; phenotype analysis under nutrient treatments
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphorus deficiency; nitrogen deficiency; adequate N and P supply; severe nutrient stress
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for effector constructs in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Diagnostic allele and KASP marker enable marker-assisted selection; useful for breeding cultivars with visible nutrient-deficiency indicators; can guide engineering of anthocyanin/purple pigmentation by deploying functional TaMYB-D7b
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cultivar 2174 carrying functional allele is described as highly sensitive to N and P deficiency, with leaf burn/wither under combined severe deficiency; purple color should not be interpreted as sufficiency trait without verifying allele presence
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: QTL mapping with SSR markers and Illumina Infinium 9K iSelect SNP genotyping; qPCR expression analysis; no public accession for omics data, data available on request
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also edited TaMYB-A7 and TaMYB-B7 together with TaMYB-D7 as TaMYB7 homoeologs; however the key natural functional allele linked to phenotype is TaMYB-D7b. The critical amino acid polymorphism is Gly-50 in TaMYB-D7b versus Ser-50 in TaMYB-D7a. Yeast autoactivation did not differ between alleles, suggesting altered DNA-binding specificity/affinity rather than general activation capacity.
